classDescription: A master of martial combat and weapon versatility.
roleIdentity: Durable frontline warrior with scalable multi-attack output.

fighter-fighting-style.summary: Lock in a permanent combat specialty that tells the player how this fighter prefers to win exchanges.
fighter-fighting-style.description: Fighting Style is one of the clearest identity levers on the fighter chassis. It should be obvious from the sheet whether the character is built around defense, archery, dueling, great weapons, or another weapon plan, because that decision colors almost every later choice.

fighter-second-wind.summary: Catch your breath and restore a meaningful chunk of staying power without surrendering your whole turn.
fighter-second-wind.description: Second Wind is the fighter’s quick self-stabilizer. It is there so the class can keep operating through chip damage, survive one more exchange, and avoid feeling fragile just because the healer is busy elsewhere.
fighter-second-wind.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.
fighter-second-wind.effect: Immediate self-healing and a steadier front-line presence.

fighter-weapon-mastery.summary: Your weapons do more than deal damage — they carry rider properties that reward choosing the right tool for the job.
fighter-weapon-mastery.description: Weapon Mastery is one of the features that makes the modern fighter feel technical instead of empty. Different weapons now imply different control, pressure, or positioning lines, so the sheet should make those mastered choices visible and easy to understand.

fighter-action-surge-1-use.summary: Explode a turn with an extra action when tempo matters more than patience.
fighter-action-surge-1-use.description: Action Surge is one of the fighter’s signature power spikes. Use it when an extra action changes the whole round: finish a target, reposition and still attack, cast and attack as an Eldritch Knight, or seize the opening before the enemy recovers.
fighter-action-surge-1-use.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.
fighter-action-surge-1-use.effect: Gain one extra action on your turn.

fighter-tactical-mind.summary: Turn disciplined focus into better clutch checks when the group needs someone to solve a problem cleanly.
fighter-tactical-mind.description: Tactical Mind exists so the fighter contributes outside raw attack math. It is a reminder that this class is not only muscle; it is trained battlefield judgment that can turn an important check from failure into success.

fighter-martial-archetype-subclass.summary: Your archetype decides what kind of fighter you really are beyond the base attack engine.
fighter-martial-archetype-subclass.description:
Martial Archetype is the subclass gate for fighter. This is where the class becomes a Battle Master, Champion, Eldritch Knight, or Psi Warrior and starts playing with a distinct tactical identity.

The sheet should treat this as a major fork because later subclass features are what stop high-level fighters from feeling like only “more attacks.”

fighter-indomitable-1-use.summary: Refuse a bad save result when the fight is too important to lose to one failed defense.
fighter-indomitable-1-use.description: Indomitable is the fighter’s answer to swingy saving throws. When a crippling effect would take you out of the scene, this feature gives you another chance to hold the line and keep acting like the martial anchor of the team.
fighter-indomitable-1-use.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Long Rest.

fighter-tactical-shift.summary: Reposition aggressively without completely giving up your turn economy.
fighter-tactical-shift.description: Tactical Shift makes the fighter feel more active in real maps and not just in a damage spreadsheet. It is about moving to the square that matters while still keeping offensive pressure online.

fighter-studied-attacks.summary: Longer exchanges reward your experience as you read patterns and convert pressure into more reliable offense.
fighter-studied-attacks.description: Studied Attacks is a veteran-fighter feature about momentum. Over repeated rounds, the fighter should feel more certain, more punishing, and harder for enemies to survive through simple repetition.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two scores by 1 each, or take a feat instead.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-4.description: At level 4, the fighter earns their first Ability Score Improvement. Use it to push Strength or Dexterity higher for better attack and damage, boost Constitution for more HP and saving throws, or take a feat that complements your archetype and fighting style.

fighter-extra-attack-2.summary: You can now make two attacks whenever you take the Attack action, doubling your basic damage output.
fighter-extra-attack-2.description: Extra Attack (2) is one of the most important damage milestones for the fighter. At level 5, each Attack action becomes two swings, and combined with Action Surge or your Fighting Style the increase in pressure is significant. This is where fighters begin to outpace most other classes in pure attack output.
fighter-extra-attack-2.effect: Make 2 attacks whenever you take the Attack action.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-6.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two scores by 1 each, or take a feat instead.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-6.description: At level 6 fighters get a second ASI opportunity. Fighters receive more ASIs than most other classes, so this is a chance to lock in a key offensive stat or take a combat feat that defines the character's tactical identity.

fighter-subclass-feature-7.summary: Your Martial Archetype grants a new ability that deepens your combat specialization.
fighter-subclass-feature-7.description: At level 7 your chosen subclass (Battle Master, Champion, Eldritch Knight, Psi Warrior, or another archetype) unlocks its first mid-tier feature. This deepens the tactical identity set at level 3 and gives the fighter a signature play pattern beyond basic attack actions.

fighter-weapon-mastery-4-weapons.summary: You can now apply Weapon Mastery properties to four weapons, widening your tactical toolkit.
fighter-weapon-mastery-4-weapons.description: Weapon Mastery (4 weapons) at level 7 gives you an extra mastered weapon. Each mastered weapon carries a special rider property — Push, Vex, Nick, Slow, Topple, or others — that adds control, pressure, or positioning value on top of raw damage. More mastered weapons means more situational flexibility.
fighter-weapon-mastery-4-weapons.effect: Apply Weapon Mastery to 4 weapons simultaneously.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-8.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two scores by 1 each, or take a feat instead.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-8.description: Level 8 delivers the fighter's third ASI, one more than most classes get by this point. This is a natural place to max out a primary combat stat or invest in a feat that rounds out utility, defense, or advanced combat maneuvers.

fighter-subclass-feature-10.summary: Your Martial Archetype grants another advanced ability, solidifying your combat identity at mid-tier.
fighter-subclass-feature-10.description: The level 10 subclass feature is a major identity marker. By this point your archetype should feel distinctly different from other fighters — a Battle Master has a deep maneuver repertoire, a Champion crits more often, an Eldritch Knight weaves spells and attacks together, or a Psi Warrior projects psychic force.

fighter-extra-attack-3.summary: You can now make three attacks whenever you take the Attack action.
fighter-extra-attack-3.description: Extra Attack (3) at level 11 is the first three-attack baseline in the game, a fighter-exclusive milestone at this tier. Three attacks per action means exceptional Action Surge turns and sustained DPR that very few other characters can match. Combined with Weapon Mastery riders, this level marks the fighter's entry into truly elite offensive territory.
fighter-extra-attack-3.effect: Make 3 attacks whenever you take the Attack action.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-12.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two scores by 1 each, or take a feat instead.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-12.description: The level 12 ASI is the fourth improvement, which is two more than most classes have at this point. Fighters are among the most feat-efficient classes in the game precisely because of this extra ASI density — use it to push a key stat to its ceiling or take a high-value combat or utility feat.

fighter-indomitable-2-uses.summary: You can now use Indomitable twice per long rest to deny a failed saving throw.
fighter-indomitable-2-uses.description: Indomitable (2 uses) at level 13 makes the fighter significantly harder to take down with debilitating effects. With two rerolls per long rest you can push through a crippling save in one combat and still have insurance for the next — turning what would be guaranteed shutdowns into recoverable setbacks.
fighter-indomitable-2-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Long Rest.

fighter-subclass-feature-13.summary: Your Martial Archetype unlocks a high-tier ability that sets up your endgame power.
fighter-subclass-feature-13.description: The level 13 subclass feature is the second-to-last major archetype upgrade. By this point the archetype's mechanical identity should feel complete and well-integrated — your approach to combat should read clearly from the character sheet without any explanation.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-14.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two scores by 1 each, or take a feat instead.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-14.description: Level 14 gives the fighter a fifth ASI — more than any other class. At this stage most primary stats are maxed, so this is often a great time for a feat that adds a new dimension to combat, utility, or roleplay capability.

fighter-weapon-mastery-5-weapons.summary: You can now apply Weapon Mastery properties to five weapons, maximizing your arsenal's versatility.
fighter-weapon-mastery-5-weapons.description: Weapon Mastery (5 weapons) at level 14 gives you the broadest possible coverage of mastery riders. With five weapons mastered you can adapt to almost any combat situation — close quarters, reach threats, ranged harassment, mounted combat — while always carrying a rider that creates meaningful tactical interaction.
fighter-weapon-mastery-5-weapons.effect: Apply Weapon Mastery to 5 weapons simultaneously.

fighter-subclass-feature-15.summary: Your Martial Archetype delivers its most powerful mid-to-late tier ability.
fighter-subclass-feature-15.description: The level 15 subclass feature often includes one of the archetype's most powerful abilities — a major mechanical payoff that rewards staying in the subclass all the way to this level. This feature should feel like a real capstone to the identity established at level 3.

fighter-ability-score-improvement-16.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two scores by 1 each, or take a feat instead.
fighter-ability-score-improvement-16.description: The level 16 ASI is the sixth improvement, making fighters the most stat-flexible class at this tier. Nearly every important combat stat should be maxed by now, freeing this slot for feats that round out utility, add roleplay hooks, or push into epic-tier readiness.

fighter-action-surge-2-uses.summary: You can now Action Surge twice per short or long rest, unlocking two explosive turns per encounter.
fighter-action-surge-2-uses.description: Action Surge (2 uses) at level 17 is a dramatic late-game power spike. With four attacks per action and two Action Surges available, a single short rest encounter can see the fighter deliver eight or more attacks — a burst of offense that can define an entire combat and reliably finish boss-tier enemies in a single focused round.
fighter-action-surge-2-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.
fighter-action-surge-2-uses.effect: Gain one extra action on your turn.

fighter-indomitable-3-uses.summary: You can now use Indomitable three times per long rest for sustained defensive resilience.
fighter-indomitable-3-uses.description: Indomitable (3 uses) at level 17 makes the fighter one of the most resistant classes to save-or-suck effects across an entire adventuring day. Three rerolls means you can absorb nearly every serious magical debuff the dungeon can throw at you over a full rest cycle, keeping you in the fight when lesser warriors would be incapacitated.
fighter-indomitable-3-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Long Rest.

fighter-subclass-feature-18.summary: Your Martial Archetype delivers its final pre-capstone ability.
fighter-subclass-feature-18.description: The level 18 subclass feature is the last major archetype unlock before the capstone at level 20. It rounds out the subclass kit and ensures the archetype feels complete across every combat scenario — this is where the gap between archetypes is widest and most meaningful.

fighter-epic-boon.summary: Gain an Epic Boon — a powerful feat-level upgrade that marks entry into legendary tier.
fighter-epic-boon.description: Epic Boon at level 19 is the fighter's penultimate power unlock. Epic Boons are stronger than standard feats and represent the fighter crossing into legendary martial territory. Options typically include extraordinary combat resilience, once-per-rest power resets, or signature abilities that anchor an entire build identity.

fighter-extra-attack-4.summary: You can now make four attacks whenever you take the Attack action — the pinnacle of martial offense.
fighter-extra-attack-4.description: Extra Attack (4) at level 20 is the fighter's ultimate offensive capstone, unique among all classes. Four attacks per action means that a single Attack action produces more swings than most full-attack sequences in prior editions. Combined with Action Surge (2 uses), Weapon Mastery riders, and your subclass capstone, a level 20 fighter can deliver the most concentrated burst damage of any martial class in a single combat turn.
fighter-extra-attack-4.effect: Make 4 attacks whenever you take the Attack action.

fighter-subclass-capstone.summary: Your Martial Archetype's ultimate ability defines the peak expression of your combat identity.
fighter-subclass-capstone.description: The subclass capstone at level 20 is the culmination of your chosen archetype. This ability should feel like the complete realization of the fighter identity you've been building since level 3 — a Battle Master with legendary tactical mastery, a Champion whose critical hits are devastating, an Eldritch Knight who blurs the line between warrior and wizard, or another archetype delivering its defining statement.

progressionSummary.summary: Fighting Style, Second Wind, Weapon Mastery.
Ability Score Improvement, Weapon Mastery (5 weapons).

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your weapon attacks and Athletics checks
buildTips.secondaryReason: increases your HP and Concentration saving throws
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: rarely used by Fighters, safe to set to 8-10
